meta_title: Impact Windows in Tamarac, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact windows in Tamarac, FL. Protect your home, lower insurance costs & stay code compliant. Free quotes, $0 down financing.
h1: Tamarac's Trusted Impact Window Experts — Built to Handle South Florida's Worst
intro:
Tamarac, Florida sits at the heart of Broward County, a city of established neighborhoods, long-time residents, and homes that have weathered decades of South Florida storms. From the tree-lined streets of Woodlands to the lake-front communities of Tamarac Lakes, and the golf course homes surrounding Colony West Country Club, this city is defined by residential pride and community stability. But with that history comes a sobering reality: more than half of Tamarac's homes were built before 1994, meaning they predate the sweeping changes to Florida's building codes that came in the wake of Hurricane Andrew. That leaves a significant portion of homeowners in ZIP codes 33309, 33319, 33320, 33321, and 33351 living with original single-pane windows that were never designed to resist today's recognized wind threat of 170 miles per hour.

While Tamarac sits approximately 8 miles from the Atlantic Coast — just far enough to fall outside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — that designation does not mean the city is protected from catastrophic storm damage. Hurricane Wilma in 2005 swept through Broward County as one of the most destructive storms in modern Florida history, and Irma in 2017 reminded every inland community that wind damage does not respect zip code boundaries. With average home values hovering around $300,000, a single hurricane season can threaten what is, for most Tamarac families, their single most important financial asset. Impact windows are no longer a luxury upgrade — they are a foundational layer of home protection that also delivers measurable financial returns through insurance savings and energy efficiency.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we have spent years serving Tamarac homeowners exactly like you — middle-income families who want the highest level of protection without overpaying for it. Our team understands the specific construction styles common in Mainlands and Colony West, the permit process with the City of Tamarac Building Division, and the product standards required under Florida Building Code for Broward County. We bring 60-plus years of combined installation experience, 100% in-house crews, and financing options starting as low as $87 per month with zero down. When Tamarac homeowners want the job done right the first time, they call the Kings of Service.

section_why.h2: Why Tamarac Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
Choosing an impact window contractor is not simply about picking a product off a shelf — it is about trusting a team to protect your family, navigate local permitting, and stand behind their work long after the last window is installed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built a reputation across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties as the contractor that treats every project like it belongs to someone in their own family. With an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau and more than 500 completed homes throughout South Florida, our track record speaks before we ever knock on your door.

What truly separates us from the competition in Tamarac is our commitment to 100% in-house installation. We do not subcontract your project to crews we cannot vouch for. Every installer who shows up at your home — whether you live off McNab Road near Tamarac Park or in one of the sprawling Woodlands subdivisions — is a trained, vetted Premier Impact Windows & Roofing employee. Our combined team carries over 60 years of hands-on experience with impact-rated window systems, and they are intimately familiar with the construction techniques and framing styles common in Broward County homes built in the 1970s, 1980s, and early 1990s.

We also understand that Tamarac is a community built on practical value. Our customers are not looking for flashy sales tactics — they want honest assessments, transparent pricing, and a company that will be reachable after the project is complete. That is why we handle every step of the process: initial consultation, product selection, permit filing with the City of Tamarac Building Division, installation, inspection coordination, and final walkthrough. No handoffs, no confusion, no surprises on your invoice. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is the full-service solution Tamarac homeowners deserve.

Our financing options were specifically designed with communities like Tamarac in mind. We offer plans starting at just $87 per month with $0 down, making it realistic for homeowners across the income spectrum to afford the protection their homes need. Combined with the insurance savings and energy cost reductions that typically follow impact window installation, many of our customers find that the project pays for itself over time — turning a necessary home improvement into a genuine long-term investment.

section_history.h2: Tamarac's Storm History Makes the Case for Impact Windows
section_history.body:
Tamarac has faced at least 9 significant storm events since 1990, and three of those storms left lasting marks on the community's collective memory. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was the event that rewrote Florida's building codes, but Tamarac homeowners bore its wrath firsthand as Andrew's outer bands tore across Broward County, damaging roofs, shattering windows, and flooding homes that had no meaningful storm protection. That event exposed just how vulnerable the pre-1994 housing stock truly was — a vulnerability that still applies to the majority of homes standing in Tamarac today.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 delivered perhaps the most direct punch to Broward County in recent history, making landfall on the Gulf Coast and racing east with sustained winds that shattered windows, peeled away roof coverings, and left communities from Lauderhill to Coconut Creek without power for weeks. The damage tally in Broward County alone reached into the hundreds of millions of dollars, and Tamarac neighborhoods like Tamarac Lakes and Mainlands saw significant property damage. Then came Irma in September 2017 — a storm that humbled every homeowner who thought inland properties were safe from serious wind exposure. Irma's track brought tropical storm and hurricane-force gusts to nearly every corner of South Florida, demonstrating that no community in this region is truly beyond the reach of a major Atlantic hurricane.

The pattern is clear: Tamarac is not an if-storm-hits scenario — it is a when, and how prepared will you be. Impact windows represent the most effective single upgrade a homeowner can make to dramatically reduce the risk of catastrophic wind and water intrusion during the next major storm event. Homes in Woodlands and Colony West that have already made this upgrade saw meaningfully better outcomes during Irma than their neighbors who had not. That firsthand local evidence carries more weight than any marketing brochure.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Windows in Tamarac
section_code.body:
Tamarac falls within Broward County's jurisdiction under the Florida Building Code, which mandates that all replacement windows and exterior glazing meet specific wind-resistance standards appropriate for the region's designated wind speed. Although Tamarac does not fall within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation reserved for Miami-Dade and portions of Broward closest to the coast — the city is still subject to a 170 mph design wind speed requirement. That means every impact window installed in a Tamarac home must be engineered, tested, and certified to perform under those conditions. This is not a voluntary upgrade standard; it is a legally enforceable code requirement applicable to any permitted window replacement project.

The permitting process for impact window installation in Tamarac runs through the City of Tamarac Building Division, which requires submission of product approval documentation, a signed and sealed permit application, and post-installation inspection before the project is officially closed. Products must carry a valid Florida Product Approval (FPA) number demonstrating compliance with the current Florida Building Code, and the installing contractor must hold an active state license. Attempting to bypass the permit process is not only illegal — it can void your homeowner's insurance coverage and create serious complications when you attempt to sell your property.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we handle every aspect of the permitting process on your behalf. Our team is fully familiar with the City of Tamarac Building Division's requirements and documentation expectations. We submit the permit application, coordinate the inspection schedule, and ensure your project is fully closed and code-compliant before we consider the job finished. For homeowners in Tamarac's older communities — where original window frames may not have been installed to any wind-resistance standard at all — working with a licensed contractor who understands these requirements is not optional. It is the foundation of a properly executed impact window project.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Windows: Premier-Grade Protection for Tamarac Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two best-in-class impact window product lines for Tamarac homeowners: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are engineered specifically for South Florida's demanding climate conditions and carry the Florida Product Approvals required for permitted installation in Broward County. These are not generic replacement windows dressed up with marketing language — they are purpose-built, rigorously tested impact systems designed to perform under sustained high winds, flying debris, and the pressure cycling that occurs during major storm events.

ESW impact windows feature laminated safety glass composed of two panes of tempered glass bonded by a durable interlayer that holds the glass together even upon impact from wind-driven debris. This construction eliminates the catastrophic pressure breach that occurs when a standard window fails during a hurricane, protecting your home's interior from wind, rain, and structural damage. ESW frames utilize multi-chamber profiles that provide superior thermal insulation and structural rigidity, and their Low-E coating technology reflects solar heat — a critical feature for Tamarac homes that carry year-round air conditioning loads and face high energy bills during South Florida's long summer season.

CWI windows bring a comparable level of performance with a slightly different product architecture that makes them an excellent fit for specific window openings and home styles common throughout Tamarac's Mainlands and Woodlands communities. CWI's multi-chamber frames reduce thermal transfer, minimize condensation, and deliver a clean, modern aesthetic that complements both the ranch-style homes and two-story residences found across Tamarac's neighborhoods. Both ESW and CWI products are available in a range of sizes, finishes, and operational styles — including single-hung, double-hung, casement, and picture configurations — giving Tamarac homeowners the flexibility to protect every opening in their home without compromising on design.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Windows Cost in Tamarac — And Why the Investment Makes Sense
section_cost.body:
For Tamarac homeowners with an average property value of around $300,000, impact window installation represents one of the highest-return home improvements available. The cost of a full-home impact window project in Tamarac typically ranges based on the size of the home, the number of openings, the window style selected, and existing frame conditions — particularly important in a community where 55% of homes predate 1994 and may require additional prep work. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides free, no-obligation quotes tailored to your specific home and circumstances, so you get an accurate number before committing to anything.

We understand that Tamarac families are making practical financial decisions, which is why our financing options were built to remove the barrier between needing impact windows and affording them. Plans start at just $87 per month with $0 down, allowing homeowners to begin protecting their property immediately without depleting savings or waiting for the perfect moment. Multiple financing structures are available depending on your credit profile and how quickly you want to pay the project off, and our team will walk you through every option without pressure.

Beyond the upfront cost, the financial case for impact windows in Tamarac is compelling. Homeowners typically see meaningful reductions in their homeowner's insurance premiums following installation — in some cases amounting to hundreds of dollars annually. Energy bills also decrease as Low-E coated laminated glass reduces solar heat gain, lowering the demand on your air conditioning system. And from a property value standpoint, impact windows are a recognized upgrade that increases appraisal value and makes your home more attractive to buyers who understand the South Florida market. Over a five to ten year horizon, many Tamarac homeowners find that impact windows effectively pay for a substantial portion of themselves.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings and Wind Mitigation Credits for Tamarac Homeowners
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediate and tangible financial benefits of installing impact windows in Tamarac is the reduction in homeowner's insurance premiums. Florida's property insurance market has been volatile in recent years, with carriers raising rates, pulling back from certain markets, or imposing stricter coverage requirements. But there is one consistent bright spot for homeowners who take proactive steps to harden their homes: wind mitigation credits. In Broward County, a certified wind mitigation inspection documenting the presence of impact-rated openings can qualify your home for significant premium discounts with most major carriers operating in the Florida market.

The Florida Department of Financial Services requires insurers to offer premium credits for verified wind mitigation features, including impact-resistant windows and doors. When a licensed wind mitigation inspector confirms that all glazed openings in your home are protected by code-compliant impact windows, your insurer is required to apply the appropriate credit to your policy. For Tamarac homeowners, where insurance premiums have climbed substantially over the past several years, these credits can translate into savings of several hundred dollars per year — savings that accumulate annually and meaningfully offset the cost of the initial installation over time.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing recommends that every Tamarac customer schedule a wind mitigation inspection through a licensed inspector after their installation is complete and permitted. We can provide guidance on the inspection process and the documentation our installation generates that supports your inspection report. Common carriers serving Broward County — including Citizens Property Insurance and numerous private market options — all recognize wind mitigation credits in their rate structures. The combination of lower premiums, reduced storm risk, and improved home value makes impact windows one of the few home improvements that generates a clear, measurable financial return year after year.

faqs.q: Do Tamarac homes really need impact windows if they're not on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Tamarac is approximately 8 miles from the Atlantic Coast, but the city has experienced significant damage from hurricanes Andrew, Wilma, and Irma — all of which produced damaging winds well inland. Broward County's 170 mph design wind speed requirement applies to Tamarac, and the Florida Building Code mandates hurricane-rated products for any permitted window replacement. Distance from the coast reduces storm surge risk, but wind damage is a real and documented threat throughout this community.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost in Tamarac, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Project costs vary based on your home's size, number of openings, and window style, but Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free in-home quotes tailored to your specific property. Financing starts at just $87 per month with $0 down, making impact windows accessible for most Tamarac homeowners. We offer multiple financing structures to fit different budgets, and many customers find that insurance savings and reduced energy bills significantly offset the monthly payment over time.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to install impact windows in Tamarac?
faqs.a: Yes. All impact window installations in Tamarac require a permit through the City of Tamarac Building Division. The permit process involves submitting product approval documentation, a licensed contractor application, and scheduling a post-installation inspection before the project is closed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf — from application submission to final inspection coordination — ensuring your project is fully code-compliant and properly documented.

faqs.q: How long does an impact window installation take for a typical Tamarac home?
faqs.a: For a typical Tamarac single-family home, the actual installation by our in-house crew generally takes one to two days depending on the number of windows and any existing frame conditions that require additional preparation. Older homes built before 1994 — which represent the majority of Tamarac's housing stock — occasionally require supplemental framing work, which we identify and communicate during your initial quote. Permit processing timelines with the City of Tamarac Building Division add some lead time before installation begins, and we keep you informed throughout the scheduling process.

faqs.q: Will impact windows lower my homeowner's insurance in Tamarac?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. Florida law requires insurance carriers to offer wind mitigation credits for homes with code-compliant impact windows covering all glazed openings. After your Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installation is complete and inspected, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can certify your home's features, and your insurer must apply the corresponding premium discount. Tamarac homeowners frequently report meaningful annual savings on their insurance premiums following impact window installation, and those savings accumulate each year you own the home.
